perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Affiliate requires a referral arrangement while Affiliate is negotiating an ISO sponsorship agreement with Network 1 and Harris Bank and this Agreement shall govern the agreement between the parties until such time that the ISO sponsorship agreement has been approval and executed in which case the Processing Agreement, ISO sponsorship Agreement, and Three Party Agreement will govern the relationship and this Agreement shall be terminated without penalty or prejudice;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Affiliate requires a referral arrangement while Affiliate is negotiating an ISO sponsorship agreement with Network 1 and Harris Bank and this Agreement shall govern the agreement between the parties until such time that the ISO sponsorship agreement has been approved and executed in which case the Processing Agreement, Partnership Agreement, and Three Party Agreement will govern the relationship and this Agreement shall be terminated without penalty or prejudice;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'ISO Sponsorship Agreement' is a defined and key element in the agreement. Changing 'ISO sponsorship Agreement' to 'Partnership Agreement' creates confusion because 'Partnership Agreement' is not a defined term in this contract, leading to uncertainty about the actual agreements that supersede this one upon execution of the ISO sponsorship. This directly contradicts the understanding of the agreement and creates uncertainty about future obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE V [TERM AND TERMINATION] TERM AND TERMINATION
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.01 TERM. The term ("Term") of this Agreement shall be for one hundred eighty days (180) from the date set forth below unless Network 1 or Visa or MasterCard or Harris Bank doesn't approve Affiliate's ISO application, in which case, the Term will be 3 years. This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless terminated by either party by providing the other with 30 days written notice that this Agreement will not be renewed or Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both rely on the term 'ISO Sponsorship agreement' to define when the initial agreement is superseded and terminated. By changing 'ISO sponsorship Agreement' to 'Partnership Agreement,' the changed text introduces an undefined term, creating ambiguity and contradicting the conditions under which the agreement terminates as defined in section 5.01. This is a clear case of misaligned terminology leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.01 CONTRACTORS. Affiliate shall use its best efforts to market and sell to commercial businesses the Services of Network 1 and Network 1's subsidiaries and to locate individuals who are willing and capable of acting as Contractors of Network 1 and Affiliate subject to the approval of all such individuals by Network 1 as set out in Section 2.01 [CONTRACTORS]. All such Contractors must process Merchant applications and transactions exclusively through Network 1. Network 1 consents to waive said exclusivity requirement with respect to specific Merchants in the event Network 1 (i) is unable to process for such specific Merchant, and (ii) the declined Merchant is not accepted for processing by a provider that Network 1 designates for specific Merchant's that are declined by Network 1 ("B Bank Source"). Affiliate shall disclose to Network 1 all information known to Affiliate with respect to the background, character, employment history, business experience and other information regarding each prospective Contractor relevant to Network 1's determination whether to accept or not accept each prospective Contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.01 AGENTS. Affiliate shall use its best efforts to market and sell to commercial businesses the Services of Network 1 and Network 1's subsidiaries and to locate individuals who are willing and capable of acting as Contractors of Network 1 and Affiliate subject to the approval of all such individuals by Network 1 as set out in Section 2.01 [CONTRACTORS]. All such Agents must process Merchant applications and transactions exclusively through Network 1. Network 1 consents to waive said exclusivity requirement with respect to specific Merchants in the event Network 1 (i) is unable to process for such specific Merchant, and (ii) the declined Merchant is not accepted for processing by a provider that Network 1 designates for specific Merchant's that are declined by Network 1 ("B Bank Source"). Affiliate shall disclose to Network 1 all information known to Affiliate with respect to the background, character, employment history, business experience and other information regarding each prospective Agent relevant to Network 1's determination whether to accept or not accept each prospective Agent.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'Contractors' to 'Agents' in section 1.01 introduces a contradiction. The agreement relies on the clear definition and understanding of 'Contractors', as specified in section 2.01, including their obligation to enter into a 'three party Contractor Agreement.' Substituting 'Agents' without providing a clear definition or specifying if they are bound by the same agreements creates confusion about their role and responsibilities, potentially leading to disputes over their contractual obligations. This contradicts the intent of having all personnel operate under the standard Contractor Agreement terms.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE I [OBLIGATIONS OF AFFILIATES] OBLIGATIONS OF AFFILIATES 1.01 CONTRACTORS
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.01 CONTRACTORS. Network 1 shall have the right, at its discretion, to accept, not accept, terminate or otherwise deal with any individuals located by Affiliate pursuant to Section 1.01 [CONTRACTORS]. All individuals identified by Affiliate shall be subject to the rules imposed by Network 1, VISA, MasterCard, NACHA, and the Member bank ("Member") utilized by Network 1, including rules pertaining to qualification disqualification, conduct and otherwise, and shall agree to enter into, and shall in fact enter into, a three party Contractor Agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit 1 as amended from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Contractors' to 'Agents' in Section 1.01 introduces a contradiction because Section 2.01 explicitly defines the requirements and obligations of 'Contractors,' including the requirement to enter into a 'three-party Contractor Agreement.' By changing the term to 'Agents,' without a corresponding definition or clarification regarding their obligations, it creates uncertainty as to whether Agents are also bound by the same agreement and rules as Contractors, leading to a conflict and ambiguity within the contract. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the agreement uses different terms without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: 5.02 TERMINATION. Agreement may be terminated prior to the conclusion of the Term by giving written notice of termination: A. By either party as a result of default by the other party under this Agreement and failure to cure said default within thirty (30) days after notice of said default is given. B. By either party in the event of insolvency, receivership, voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy or an assignment for the benefit of creditors of or by the Affiliate other than in the ordinary course of business. However, Affiliate may pledge or otherwise collateralize assets for the purpose of securing commercial loans or lines of credit in the ordinary course of business provided that such pledge is subordinate to an security interest associated with the Merchant accounts and/or losses from such accounts. C. By Affiliate in the event of any changes in the Affiliate's Fee (other than direct pass through increases related to Visa and MasterCard interchange, fees, assessments and dues, processor communication costs, and other direct increases including terminal hardware). D. By Network 1, for cause. For purpose hereof; "cause" shall consist of (i) fraud, intentional misrepresentation or negligence by Affiliate or any Contractor located by Affiliate in compiling or providing any information submitted to or relied on by Network 1 to Network 1, whether or not such fraud or misrepresentation is based on a misstatement, omission, a substantive fact, or data; (ii) intentional violations by the Affiliate or any Contractor(s) located by Affiliate of any of the rules or regulations of VISA, MasterCard, the Member Bank or Network 1; and (iii) the providing of vendor services or merchant services by Affiliate or Contractor(s) located by Affiliate which are competitive with Network 1 or without the prior written consent of Network 1, contrary to Section 1.01 [CONTRACTORS] and IV, violation of any clause of Network 1 Affiliate Office Agreement and failure to cure such violation within 30 days of notification E. By Network 1 in the event any provision of the Sales Certificate or the Code of Ethics is in breach by the Affiliate or Contractor(s) located by Affiliate. F. Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements. If this Agreement is terminated for cause, all rights of the Affiliate to future payments hereunder shall immediately terminate and Transferability will remain in place in accordance with Section 2.03 (b) [RETENTION OF CONTRACTORS] and 2.03 (c).
perturbation.changed_text: 5.02 TERMINATION. Agreement may be terminated prior to the conclusion of the Term by giving written notice of termination: A. By either party as a result of default by the other party under this Agreement and failure to cure said default within thirty (30) days after notice of said default is given. B. By either party in the event of insolvency, receivership, voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy or an assignment for the benefit of creditors of or by the Affiliate other than in the ordinary course of business. However, Affiliate may pledge or otherwise collateralize assets for the purpose of securing commercial loans or lines of credit in the ordinary course of business provided that such pledge is subordinate to an security interest associated with the Merchant accounts and/or losses from such accounts. C. By Affiliate in the event of any changes in the Affiliate's Fee (other than direct pass through increases related to Visa and MasterCard interchange, fees, assessments and dues, processor communication costs, and other direct increases including terminal hardware). D. By Network 1, for cause. For purpose hereof; "cause" shall consist of (i) fraud, intentional misrepresentation or negligence by Affiliate or any Contractor located by Affiliate in compiling or providing any information submitted to or relied on by Network 1 to Network 1, whether or not such fraud or misrepresentation is based on a misstatement, omission, a substantive fact, or data; (ii) intentional violations by the Affiliate or any Contractor(s) located by Affiliate of any of the rules or regulations of VISA, MasterCard, the Member Bank or Network 1; and (iii) the providing of vendor services or merchant services by Affiliate or Contractor(s) located by Affiliate which are competitive with Network 1 or without the prior written consent of Network 1, contrary to Section 1.01 [CONTRACTORS] and IV, violation of any clause of Network 1 Affiliate Office Agreement and failure to cure such violation within 30 days of notification E. By Network 1 in the event any provision of the Sales Certificate or the Code of Ethics is in breach by the Affiliate or Sub-Affiliate located by Affiliate. F. Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements. If this Agreement is terminated for cause, all rights of the Affiliate to future payments hereunder shall immediately terminate and Transferability will remain in place in accordance with Section 2.03 (b) [RETENTION OF CONTRACTORS] and 2.03 (c).
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'Contractor(s)' to 'Sub-Affiliate' in section 5.02 clause E introduces a contradiction. The agreement does not define or mention what constitutes a 'Sub-Affiliate', thus introducing ambiguity. The definition in the document for 'Contractor' is clear, as mentioned in section 2.01. Therefore, this directly contradicts the understanding of which parties can cause the agreement to breach due to 'Sales Certificate' or 'Code of Ethics' violations.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE V [TERM AND TERMINATION] TERM AND TERMINATION 5.02 TERMINATION
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE I [OBLIGATIONS OF AFFILIATES] OBLIGATIONS OF AFFILIATES 1.01 CONTRACTORS
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.01 CONTRACTORS. Affiliate shall use its best efforts to market and sell to commercial businesses the Services of Network 1 and Network 1's subsidiaries and to locate individuals who are willing and capable of acting as Contractors of Network 1 and Affiliate subject to the approval of all such individuals by Network 1 as set out in Section 2.01 [CONTRACTORS]. All such Contractors must process Merchant applications and transactions exclusively through Network 1. Network 1 consents to waive said exclusivity requirement with respect to specific Merchants in the event Network 1 (i) is unable to process for such specific Merchant, and (ii) the declined Merchant is not accepted for processing by a provider that Network 1 designates for specific Merchant's that are declined by Network 1 ("B Bank Source"). Affiliate shall disclose to Network 1 all information known to Affiliate with respect to the background, character, employment history, business experience and other information regarding each prospective Contractor relevant to Network 1's determination whether to accept or not accept each prospective Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Contractor(s)' to 'Sub-Affiliate' in Section 5.02(E) creates a contradiction because 'Sub-Affiliate' is not defined in the agreement, while 'Contractor' is defined in Section 1.01. This creates ambiguity regarding which parties' actions can trigger a breach under Section 5.02(E), directly conflicting with the defined understanding of 'Contractor' in the agreement, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. The contradicted text in 1.01 defines what a contractor is, giving Network 1 rights over this contractor. Changing 5.02(E) to refer to 'Sub-Affiliate' makes the meaning of the clause unclear, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
